title: AICBG (Left) to Maxilla
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/02 Dentoalveolar & Implant/AICBG (Left) to Maxilla.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: AICBG (Left) to Maxilla

Findings: 

-anterior maxillary buccal defect from sites aicbg-left-to-maxilla:toothNumber:0 with significant scaring of buccal mucosa
-anterior maxilla accessed through sulcular incision with distal release creating full thickness submucosal plane up to the nasal floor
-Previous oral antral defect appreciated with intact sinus floor but persistent bony defect along sinus floor. 

-aicbg-left-to-maxilla:text:0 anterior iliac crest was accessed through medical approach careful not dissecting off the tensor fascia latae
-pre-planned segments utilized to template tricortical block which was harvested along with medullary bone
-hip closed in layered fashion and pressure dressing applied
-tricortical block was fixated to anterior sextant with titanium mesh and aicbg-left-to-maxilla:hardwareCount:0x monocortical screws
-collagen membrane placed along bony sinus defect
-harvested medullar AICBG packed along inferior and buccal aspect of site along with PRF
-PTFE membrane placed over graft site and closed with interrupted and horizontal mattress 3-0 dyed Monocryl 

Technique:

The patient was met in the pre-op holding area. The history and physical was updated and consent verified to be signed. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. 

The patient was transferred to the operating room. The patient was placed in a supine position upon the operating room table with all pressure points relieved with padding. The patient was induced under general anesthesia, and the airway was secured with a oral endotracheal tube. The oral cavity was suctioned free of secretions and debris. The mouth was disinfected with 0.12% Chlorhexidine mouth rinse. A surgical timeout was completed. The patient was then prepped and draped in a sterile fashion, with the left hip and head/neck sites isolated. Two separate surgical stands/equipment were maintained throughout the procedure, and care was taken to not contaminate the left hip surgical site. The surgical sites were then separately anesthetized with 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine, administered via local infiltration.

Attention was directed to the anterior maxilla, where a vestibular incision was made with a 15 blade from tooth aicbg-left-to-maxilla:toothNumber:1 to aicbg-left-to-maxilla:toothNumber:2, extending over the edentulous maxillary ridge, with distobuccal vertical release at the bilateral maxillary buttress. A full thickness mucoperiosteal envelope flap was raised, and the maxilla skeletonized in LeFort-like fashion. The infraorbital neurovascular bundle was identified and protected. The submucosal flap was further developed exposing the nasal floor. The previous oral antral communication was found to be closed with healthy sinus mucosa, but still present bony defect. Site was covered with sterile towel and team rescrubbed and moved to hip. 

Attention directed to the aicbg-left-to-maxilla:text:1 anterior hip. A cutaneous and subcutaneous incision was made with a 15 blade along the aicbg-left-to-maxilla:text:2 anterior iliac crest, 2.0 cm posterior and lateral to the anterior superior iliac spine, per standard protocol. The incision measured approximately aicbg-left-to-maxilla:measurement:0 cm in length. Electrocautery was then used to carry the incision down to periosteum through Scarpa’s fascia and the investing fascia/aponeurosis of the external oblique and tensor fascia lata. The iliac crest periosteum was then incised with electrocautery, and subperiosteal dissection was performed along the length of the incision and medially down the anterior iliac crest. Malleable and “Cobra” retractors were placed to protect the abdominal contents. Along the medial aspect of the iliac crest, a reciprocating saw was then used under copious irrigation to make the vertical osteotomies and piezo for horizontal osteotomies. Chisel osteotomes were then use to free the tricortical block graft. Spoon curettes were then used to remove medullary bone from the anterior iliac crest. The site was irrigated thoroughly, packed with Arista and thrombin soaked gel foam, and hemostasis was insured. The site was then closed in a layered fashion, with 3-0 vicryl sutures approximating the periosteum and muscular aponeurosis, 4-0 vicryl sutures for deep dermal closure, and 4-0 [Prolene/Monocryl — original read "Prolene Monocryl"; specify the suture used] primary closure of the skin with tacking 5-0 plain gut. Dermabond was applied. The wound was then dressed with an island gauze/tegaderm dressing. 

Next, attention moved to the maxilla. The bony margins of the recipient site were then smoothed and block contoured for passive fit. Site copiously irrigated with sterile saline. Block fixated with aicbg-left-to-maxilla:hardwareCount:1x monocortical screws (aicbg-left-to-maxilla:hardwareCount:2 within the block and medial to aicbg-left-to-maxilla:toothNumber:3) with a trimmed Synthes aicbg-left-to-maxilla:text:3 titanium mesh. Medullar bone was then packed along inferior and buccal aspect of the maxillary defect mixed with PRF. PTFE membrane was the seated along the palatal aspect and draped over the graft site. Subperiosteal releasing incisions develop along the buccal aspect of the flap allowing tension free closure. The site was then closed with horizontal mattress and interrupted 3-0 dyed Monocryl sutures. 

The oral cavity and posterior oropharynx were thoroughly suctioned with a Yankauer suction tip,. An orogastric tube was passed to decompress the stomach. The face was cleaned with moist ray-tec gauze. This concluded the surgical procedures. The patient was turned over to the care of the anesthesia team. The patient emerged from anesthesia in the main operating room and transferred to the PACU with stable vital signs and spontaneous respirations.

title: All-On-6 Maxilla
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/02 Dentoalveolar & Implant/All-On-6 Maxilla.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: All-On-6 Maxilla

Findings: 



Maxillary all-on-6. Prosthodontist provided out of bounds guide. Tuberosity to tuberosity full thickness mucoperiosteal flap was elevated. Extraction of all remaining maxillary teeth completed. all-on-6-maxilla:side:0 maxillary sinus exposure after extraction of tooth all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:0, no perforation noted. All implants were removed/explanted. Extraction site curetted and irrigated with sterile saline. Alveoloplasty was completed with approximately all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:0 mm of alveolar reduction anterior and all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:1 mm of alveolar reduction posterior. Six Nobel Active implants were placed in the maxilla as follows:
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:1: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:0 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:2 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:3 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:4)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:2: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:1 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:5 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:6 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:7)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:3: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:2 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:8 Ncm, (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:9 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:10)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:4: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:3 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:11 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:12 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:13)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:5: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:4 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:14 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:15 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:16)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:6: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:5 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:17 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:18 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:19)

all-on-6-maxilla:side:1 sinus floor exposure during placement of implant all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:7, which was then covered with collagen membrane at the end of the case.
Multi-unit abutments were placed on all implants, contoured to the bone profile per the out of bounds guide. A smart denture conversion technique utilized by the prosthodontist.
all-on-6-maxilla:side:2 maxillary sinus covered with gel foam, incision closed primarily by 3-0 Vicryl in single interrupted fashion. Tissue around abutment trimmed by tissue punch. The Prosth team then converted and delivered immediate denture to pt, screw covered by light body PVS impression material.
10 cc of Exparel was infiltrated at the conclusion of the case.

Technique:

The patient was greeted in the operating room holding area where identification was verified and the health history was updated. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. The procedure was reviewed, and the consents and surgical sites were verified. The patient was transported to the operating room and placed on the operating room table in supine position. General anesthesia was induced and a nasoendotracheal tube was placed and secured. The eyes were protected. The patient was then draped in sterile fashion for a surgical procedure of the oral cavity. A surgical time-out was completed. Moist throat pack placed. 

Bilateral Maxillary Alveoloplasty/Extraction of remaining maxillary teeth:
Attention was directed to the patient’s maxilla, where a #15 blade was used to make a crestal incision, along the palatal aspect of the alveolar ridge, from the right to left first molar regions, with distal releases raising a full thickness mucoperiosteal flap. The maxilla was skeletonized in a LeFort procedure-like fashion, identifying the bilateral piriform rims. The nasal floor was conservatively dissected bilaterally to the extent of the anterior wall of the maxillary sinus. The palatal gingiva was also reflected in a subperiosteal plane. All remaining teeth and root tips were extracted with luxators, rongeurs, and dental forceps. Sinus membrane was noted to be exposed but intact at extraction socket all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:8. The maxillary implants all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:9 were removed with dental forceps and a rongeurs. The reduction guide was seated and depth of alveoloplasty marked with a sterile golf pencil. A pineapple bur under copious irrigation was then used to complete maxillary alveoloplasty in x2 quadrants (all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:20 mm reduction). The prosthodontic team was present and verified adequate reduction of alveolar bone. All maxillary sites were then irrigated. 

Maxillary Implants x6:
Attention was then turned to implant placement. The implant osteotomy sites were planned at positions all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:10 to optimize prosthetic support and biomechanical distribution for an All-on-X maxillary fixed prosthesis. The anterior implants at sites all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:11 were planned with angulation toward the piriform rim to maximize anterior-posterior spread. The posterior implants at sites all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:12 were planned with angulation to maximize anterior-posterior spread and avoid the maxillary sinus. An out-of-bounds guide was used to ensure appropriate implant placement under direction of the prosthodontic team. 
Attention directed to implant sites all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:13. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:6mm dimension osteotomy at site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:14. Site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:15 was angled toward the piriform and verified tactually through the piriform nasal floor dissection. A Nobel Active all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:7mm implant was then placed and torqued to all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:21 Ncm. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:8mm dimension osteotomy at site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:16. Biomet all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:9mm implant was then placed and torqued to all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:22 Ncm. 

Attention directed to implant site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:17. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:10mm dimension osteotomy. The sinus was encountered during the initial osteotomies and the site was prepped just under the depth of the sinus floor. Prior to implant placement a Helitape membrane was tucked within the osteotomy and the final all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:11mm implant was placed within the osteotomy and torqued to all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:23 Ncm. 

Attention directed to implant sites all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:18. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:12mm dimension osteotomy at site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:19. A Nobel Active all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:13mm implant was then placed and torqued to all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:24 Ncm. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:14mm dimension osteotomy at site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:20. Biomet all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:15mm implant was then placed and torqued to all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:25 Ncm at site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:21. 


Attention directed to implant site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:22. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:16mm dimension osteotomy at site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:23. The osteotomy was noted to have impinged on the nasal floor through tactile sensation through piriform. The osteotomy was then moved anteriorly and redirected posteriorly toward the piriform and underprepped all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:17mm dimension osteotomy at site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:24. A Nobel Active all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:18mm implant was then placed and torqued to all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:26 Ncm. 

Cover screws were then placed over implants all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:25. Profiling of the bone around the implants was accomplished with a #4 round diamond bur. The cover screws were removed and placed on implants all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:26. Profiling was accomplished in a similar fashion. 

Multi-unit abutments were then connected to each implant and torqued to manufacturer specifications. The angled multi-unit abutments were placed and secured at 15 Ncm. The abutments were fitted to the existing maxillary prosthesis to verify passive fit and appropriate alignment. Gel foam was placed within site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:27 and a collagen membrane placed on the all-on-6-maxilla:side:3 nasal floor. 


Six Nobel Active implants with regular platform (RP) connections were placed as follows:
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:28: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:19 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:27 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:28 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:29)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:29: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:20 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:30 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:31 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:32)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:30: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:21 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:33 Ncm, (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:34 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:35)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:31: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:22 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:36 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:37 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:38)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:32: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:23 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:39 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:40 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:41)
all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:33: RP all-on-6-maxilla:hardwareDim:24 mm, all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:42 Ncm (MUA: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:43 degrees, H: all-on-6-maxilla:measurement:44)


Prosthesis pickup: 
The patient was then handed over to the prosthodontic team where an impression pickup was captured of the implants. The prosthesis was fabricated on the back table. See prosthodontic note for details. 

Closure:
Attention re-directed to the maxilla. All sites were then irrigated with copious sterile normal saline. A collagen membrane was placed at the all-on-6-maxilla:side:4 nasal floor. Gel foam was packed within extraction socket all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:34 and mucosa reapproximated with 3-0 Vicryl sutures in a horizontal mattress and figure of eight style fashion. The mucosal flaps were approximated initially with 3-0 vicryl tacking interrupted sutures. At site all-on-6-maxilla:toothNumber:35 the palatal tissue notably covered the entirety of the implant and implant uncovered using a 4.0 biopsy tissue punch. Excess gingival tissue was trimmed with a #15 blade. All tissue was noted to be able to seat passively around multiunit abutments. 

Copious irrigation of oral cavity was performed and suctioned. Exparel was administered for postop analgesia. The patient was then handed over to the prosthodontic team for delivery of the provisional prosthesis. See prosthodontic note. 

Throat pack removed. Orogastric tube passed to decompress the stomach. The face was cleaned with moist gauze. Patient emerged from anesthesia and was extubated in the operating room. The patient was then transferred to the PACU for routine postoperative recovery.

all-on-6-maxilla:text:0 was present throughout the duration of the procedure.

flags.text: CONFIRM — 2026-06-14 internal inconsistencies in the original; reconcile to the actual case before signing: (1) Findings/closing summary list all six implants as Nobel Active, but the technique narrative places Biomet implants at #5 and #12. (2) Torque values differ between the summary and the narrative (e.g., #3 35 vs 50 Ncm; #8 70 vs 30 Ncm; #14 35 vs 50 Ncm). (3) #8 listed as 5.0x10 in the summary vs 5x8 in the narrative. Choose the true intraoperative values throughout.

rawBody:
PROCEDURE: All-On-6 Maxilla

Findings: 

[CONFIRM — 2026-06-14 internal inconsistencies in the original; reconcile to the actual case before signing: (1) Findings/closing summary list all six implants as Nobel Active, but the technique narrative places Biomet implants at #5 and #12. (2) Torque values differ between the summary and the narrative (e.g., #3 35 vs 50 Ncm; #8 70 vs 30 Ncm; #14 35 vs 50 Ncm). (3) #8 listed as 5.0x10 in the summary vs 5x8 in the narrative. Choose the true intraoperative values throughout.]

Maxillary all-on-6. Prosthodontist provided out of bounds guide. Tuberosity to tuberosity full thickness mucoperiosteal flap was elevated. Extraction of all remaining maxillary teeth completed. [R/L] maxillary sinus exposure after extraction of tooth [#__], no perforation noted. All implants were removed/explanted. Extraction site curetted and irrigated with sterile saline. Alveoloplasty was completed with approximately [X] mm of alveolar reduction anterior and [X] mm of alveolar reduction posterior. Six Nobel Active implants were placed in the maxilla as follows:
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm, (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])

[R/L] sinus floor exposure during placement of implant [#__], which was then covered with collagen membrane at the end of the case.
Multi-unit abutments were placed on all implants, contoured to the bone profile per the out of bounds guide. A smart denture conversion technique utilized by the prosthodontist.
[R/L] maxillary sinus covered with gel foam, incision closed primarily by 3-0 Vicryl in single interrupted fashion. Tissue around abutment trimmed by tissue punch. The Prosth team then converted and delivered immediate denture to pt, screw covered by light body PVS impression material.
10 cc of Exparel was infiltrated at the conclusion of the case.

Technique:

The patient was greeted in the operating room holding area where identification was verified and the health history was updated. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. The procedure was reviewed, and the consents and surgical sites were verified. The patient was transported to the operating room and placed on the operating room table in supine position. General anesthesia was induced and a nasoendotracheal tube was placed and secured. The eyes were protected. The patient was then draped in sterile fashion for a surgical procedure of the oral cavity. A surgical time-out was completed. Moist throat pack placed. 

Bilateral Maxillary Alveoloplasty/Extraction of remaining maxillary teeth:
Attention was directed to the patient’s maxilla, where a #15 blade was used to make a crestal incision, along the palatal aspect of the alveolar ridge, from the right to left first molar regions, with distal releases raising a full thickness mucoperiosteal flap. The maxilla was skeletonized in a LeFort procedure-like fashion, identifying the bilateral piriform rims. The nasal floor was conservatively dissected bilaterally to the extent of the anterior wall of the maxillary sinus. The palatal gingiva was also reflected in a subperiosteal plane. All remaining teeth and root tips were extracted with luxators, rongeurs, and dental forceps. Sinus membrane was noted to be exposed but intact at extraction socket [#__]. The maxillary implants [#__] were removed with dental forceps and a rongeurs. The reduction guide was seated and depth of alveoloplasty marked with a sterile golf pencil. A pineapple bur under copious irrigation was then used to complete maxillary alveoloplasty in x2 quadrants ([X] mm reduction). The prosthodontic team was present and verified adequate reduction of alveolar bone. All maxillary sites were then irrigated. 

Maxillary Implants x6:
Attention was then turned to implant placement. The implant osteotomy sites were planned at positions [#__] to optimize prosthetic support and biomechanical distribution for an All-on-X maxillary fixed prosthesis. The anterior implants at sites [#__] were planned with angulation toward the piriform rim to maximize anterior-posterior spread. The posterior implants at sites [#__] were planned with angulation to maximize anterior-posterior spread and avoid the maxillary sinus. An out-of-bounds guide was used to ensure appropriate implant placement under direction of the prosthodontic team. 
Attention directed to implant sites [#__]. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. Site [#__] was angled toward the piriform and verified tactually through the piriform nasal floor dissection. A Nobel Active [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. Biomet [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm. 

Attention directed to implant site [#__]. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy. The sinus was encountered during the initial osteotomies and the site was prepped just under the depth of the sinus floor. Prior to implant placement a Helitape membrane was tucked within the osteotomy and the final [diameter]x[length]mm implant was placed within the osteotomy and torqued to [X] Ncm. 

Attention directed to implant sites [#__]. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. A Nobel Active [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. Biomet [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm at site [#__]. 


Attention directed to implant site [#__]. The Nobel implant system was followed to develop an underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. The osteotomy was noted to have impinged on the nasal floor through tactile sensation through piriform. The osteotomy was then moved anteriorly and redirected posteriorly toward the piriform and underprepped [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy at site [#__]. A Nobel Active [diameter]x[length]mm implant was then placed and torqued to [X] Ncm. 

Cover screws were then placed over implants [#__]. Profiling of the bone around the implants was accomplished with a #4 round diamond bur. The cover screws were removed and placed on implants [#__]. Profiling was accomplished in a similar fashion. 

Multi-unit abutments were then connected to each implant and torqued to manufacturer specifications. The angled multi-unit abutments were placed and secured at 15 Ncm. The abutments were fitted to the existing maxillary prosthesis to verify passive fit and appropriate alignment. Gel foam was placed within site [#__] and a collagen membrane placed on the [R/L] nasal floor. 


Six Nobel Active implants with regular platform (RP) connections were placed as follows:
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm, (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])
[#__]: RP [diameter]x[length] mm, [X] Ncm (MUA: [X] degrees, H: [X])


Prosthesis pickup: 
The patient was then handed over to the prosthodontic team where an impression pickup was captured of the implants. The prosthesis was fabricated on the back table. See prosthodontic note for details. 

Closure:
Attention re-directed to the maxilla. All sites were then irrigated with copious sterile normal saline. A collagen membrane was placed at the [R/L] nasal floor. Gel foam was packed within extraction socket [#__] and mucosa reapproximated with 3-0 Vicryl sutures in a horizontal mattress and figure of eight style fashion. The mucosal flaps were approximated initially with 3-0 vicryl tacking interrupted sutures. At site [#__] the palatal tissue notably covered the entirety of the implant and implant uncovered using a 4.0 biopsy tissue punch. Excess gingival tissue was trimmed with a #15 blade. All tissue was noted to be able to seat passively around multiunit abutments. 

Copious irrigation of oral cavity was performed and suctioned. Exparel was administered for postop analgesia. The patient was then handed over to the prosthodontic team for delivery of the provisional prosthesis. See prosthodontic note. 

Throat pack removed. Orogastric tube passed to decompress the stomach. The face was cleaned with moist gauze. Patient emerged from anesthesia and was extubated in the operating room. The patient was then transferred to the PACU for routine postoperative recovery.

[Attending] was present throughout the duration of the procedure.

title: Arthroscopy Bilateral with PRF Injection
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/05 TMJ/Arthroscopy Bilateral with PRF Injection.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Arthroscopy Bilateral with PRF Injection

FINDINGS: Bilateral evidence of chondromalacia (Grade arthroscopy-bilateral-with-prf-injection:blank:0), marked hyperemic synovitis, and multiple intra-articular adhesions. Bilateral anterior displacement of the articular discs without spontaneous reduction.

The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. Peripheral blood was drawn for PRF processing prior to induction and centrifuged according to standard protocols to yield injectable liquid PRF.

The patient was escorted to the operating room and placed in the supine position on the operating table. Anesthesia monitors were placed. The patient was placed under anesthesia and intubated via nasal endotracheal tube without incident. The bilateral face and ears were prepped and draped per standard sterile protocol. Cotton pledgets were placed in the bilateral external auditory meatuses.

Attention was first directed to the right TMJ. Standard bony landmarks (canthal-tragal line, glenoid fossa) were palpated and marked. The superior joint space was insufflated with arthroscopy-bilateral-with-prf-injection:measurement:0 cc of Lactated Ringer's. A stab incision was made, and a blunt trocar and cannula were inserted. The arthroscope was introduced. Diagnostic arthroscopy revealed significant synovitis, adhesions, and disc displacement. A working portal was established anteriorly via triangulation. Comprehensive lysis of the intra-articular adhesions was performed. The joint was heavily lavaged with arthroscopy-bilateral-with-prf-injection:measurement:1 cc of Lactated Ringer's solution. Upon completion of lysis and lavage, arthroscopy-bilateral-with-prf-injection:measurement:2 cc of the prepared i-PRF was injected directly into the retrodiscal tissues and superior joint space under visualization. The cannula was removed, and the puncture site was closed with a 6-0 Prolene suture.

Attention was then directed to the left TMJ, where an identical procedure was performed utilizing insufflation, introduction of the arthroscope, establishment of a working portal, lysis of adhesions, and copious lavage with arthroscopy-bilateral-with-prf-injection:measurement:3 cc of Lactated Ringer's. Left-sided findings were consistent with the right. The remaining arthroscopy-bilateral-with-prf-injection:measurement:4 cc of i-PRF was injected into the left superior joint space. The left puncture site was closed with a 6-0 Prolene suture.

The external auditory pledgets were removed bilaterally, and the tympanic membranes were confirmed intact. The patient was awakened, extubated without incident, and transported to the PACU in stable condition with stable vital signs.

title: Arthroscopy Unilateral with PRF Injection
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/05 TMJ/Arthroscopy Unilateral with PRF Injection.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Arthroscopy Unilateral with PRF Injection

FINDINGS: Grade arthroscopy-unilateral-with-prf-injection:blank:0 chondromalacia of the articular eminence and condylar head. Moderate to severe synovitis. Fibrillation of the superior joint space and anterior displacement of the articular disc. Adhesions noted in the anterior pouch.

The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. Prior to induction, peripheral blood was drawn into specific PRF tubes and centrifuged per standard protocol to yield injectable PRF (i-PRF).

The patient was escorted to the operating room and placed in the supine position on the operating table. Anesthesia monitors were placed. The patient was placed under anesthesia and intubated with a nasal endotracheal tube without incident. The arthroscopy-unilateral-with-prf-injection:side:0 face and ear were prepped and draped per standard sterile protocol. A cotton pledget was placed in the arthroscopy-unilateral-with-prf-injection:side:1 external auditory meatus.

The standard canthal-tragal line and articular fossa landmarks were identified and marked. The arthroscopy-unilateral-with-prf-injection:side:2 TMJ superior joint space was insufflated with arthroscopy-unilateral-with-prf-injection:measurement:0 cc of Lactated Ringer's solution using a 27-gauge needle, which returned freely, confirming intracapsular placement. A small stab incision was made over the posterior puncture site. The trocar and cannula were introduced into the superior joint space. The arthroscope was introduced, and diagnostic sweep of the joint was performed, revealing the findings noted above.

An anterior portal was then established using a triangulation technique. Thorough lysis of adhesions within the anterior recess and superior joint space was performed using blunt instrumentation. The joint was copiously lavaged with approximately arthroscopy-unilateral-with-prf-injection:measurement:1 cc of Lactated Ringer’s solution.

Following successful lysis and lavage, the previously prepared liquid i-PRF (approx. arthroscopy-unilateral-with-prf-injection:measurement:2 cc) was injected directly into the superior joint space and retrodiscal tissues under direct visualization. The instruments were withdrawn. Hemostasis was achieved with manual pressure. A single 6-0 Prolene suture was placed at the trocar site. The external auditory meatus pledget was removed, and the ear was examined.

The patient was awakened, extubated without incident, and transported to the PACU in stable condition with stable vital signs.

rawBody:
PROCEDURE: Arthroscopy Unilateral with PRF Injection

FINDINGS: Grade [__] chondromalacia of the articular eminence and condylar head. Moderate to severe synovitis. Fibrillation of the superior joint space and anterior displacement of the articular disc. Adhesions noted in the anterior pouch.

The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. Prior to induction, peripheral blood was drawn into specific PRF tubes and centrifuged per standard protocol to yield injectable PRF (i-PRF).

The patient was escorted to the operating room and placed in the supine position on the operating table. Anesthesia monitors were placed. The patient was placed under anesthesia and intubated with a nasal endotracheal tube without incident. The [R/L] face and ear were prepped and draped per standard sterile protocol. A cotton pledget was placed in the [R/L] external auditory meatus.

The standard canthal-tragal line and articular fossa landmarks were identified and marked. The [R/L] TMJ superior joint space was insufflated with [X] cc of Lactated Ringer's solution using a 27-gauge needle, which returned freely, confirming intracapsular placement. A small stab incision was made over the posterior puncture site. The trocar and cannula were introduced into the superior joint space. The arthroscope was introduced, and diagnostic sweep of the joint was performed, revealing the findings noted above.

An anterior portal was then established using a triangulation technique. Thorough lysis of adhesions within the anterior recess and superior joint space was performed using blunt instrumentation. The joint was copiously lavaged with approximately [X] cc of Lactated Ringer’s solution.

Following successful lysis and lavage, the previously prepared liquid i-PRF (approx. [X] cc) was injected directly into the superior joint space and retrodiscal tissues under direct visualization. The instruments were withdrawn. Hemostasis was achieved with manual pressure. A single 6-0 Prolene suture was placed at the trocar site. The external auditory meatus pledget was removed, and the ear was examined.

The patient was awakened, extubated without incident, and transported to the PACU in stable condition with stable vital signs.

title: Blepharoplasty Upper Bilateral
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/06 Cosmetic & Facial/Blepharoplasty Upper Bilateral.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Blepharoplasty Upper Bilateral

FINDINGS: Redundant skin and orbicularis oculi muscle of the bilateral upper eyelids. Herniation of the medial fat compartments bilaterally. Excellent hemostasis at the conclusion of the case.

The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. In the preoperative area, the patient was marked in a seated, upright position. The inferior limb of the incision was marked within the natural supratarsal crease, approximately 8-10 mm above the ciliary margin. The superior limb was marked using a pinch test to identify redundant skin, ensuring at least 20 mm of skin remained between the inferior brow margin and the ciliary margin for appropriate closure.

The patient was escorted to the operating room and placed in the supine position on the operating table. Anesthesia monitors were placed. The patient was placed under [general anesthesia and intubated via oral endotracheal tube / MAC sedation / local with monitored sedation — specify anesthesia type and airway; note later text says "extubated," implying GETA] without incident. Corneal shields were placed bilaterally after the application of ophthalmic lubricating ointment. The face was prepped and draped per standard sterile protocol.

Local anesthesia consisting of 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was infiltrated into the planned surgical sites of the bilateral upper eyelids. Following appropriate time for hemostasis, attention was directed to the right upper eyelid. A #15 blade was used to incise along the previously marked lines. A strip of redundant skin and a conservative strip of the underlying orbicularis oculi muscle were excised using Westcott scissors. The orbital septum was carefully opened medially, and the medial fat pad was identified. The fat pad was gently teased out, clamped, excised, and the stump cauterized with bipolar electrocautery before retracting back into the orbit. Meticulous hemostasis was achieved throughout the surgical bed using bipolar electrocautery. The incision was then closed utilizing a 6-0 Prolene suture in a running continuous fashion.

Attention was then directed to the left upper eyelid, where the identical procedure was performed. Bilateral symmetry was assessed and noted to be excellent. The corneal shields were removed, and the eyes were cleaned. Erythromycin ophthalmic ointment was applied to the suture lines. Ice packs were placed over the eyes. The patient was awakened, extubated without incident, and transported to the PACU in stable condition with stable vital signs.

title: Bone Graft Maxilla Anterior
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/02 Dentoalveolar & Implant/Bone Graft Maxilla Anterior.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Bone Graft Maxilla Anterior

 

FINDINGS: Anterior maxillary cystic/dehiscence defect accessed via sulcular incision (bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:toothNumber:0 to bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:toothNumber:1) with full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap to the piriform rim. bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:measurement:0 cc serous fluid aspirated through a pinpoint dehiscence and sent for pathology. Cystic lining enucleated in full with curettes and submitted for permanent pathology. Intact sinus floor confirmed (no fluid return per nares). bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:measurement:1 cc DBX putty placed in the defect, covered with a bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:measurement:2 cm x bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:measurement:3 cm Duragen collagen membrane. Watertight closure with 3-0 Vicryl. Hemostasis achieved.



 

TECHNIQUE

The patient was met in the preoperative holding area.  The plan for the surgery was reviewed with the patient.  The history and physical examination was updated.  Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient, including possible jaw fracture, reconstruction of mandible, maxillomandibular fixation, potential numbness and re-operation.  Consent was verified.  All questions were answered.

The patient was then transported to the operating room and placed in the supine position on the operating room table.  General anesthesia was induced with an oral endotracheal tube taped to the left side of pt's mouth and the patient was turned over to the operating team, the eyes protected and pressure points were padded and checked.  The incision site was then marked. The patient was prepped and draped in the standard fashion. A surgical time-out was performed.

 

Attention was then drawn to the patient's anterior maxilla. 18g sharp on 10cc syringe was used to aspirate through existing pinpoint dehiscence to withdraw bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:measurement:4 cc of serous fluid for sample to be sent to pathology. 1% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was administered in surgical sites. #15 was used for sulcular incision from distal of bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:toothNumber:2 to distal of bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:toothNumber:3. Full thickness mucoperiosteum flap was elevated and a 9-molt was used to progress a subperiosteal dissection to expose the maxilla to the piriform rim and around the cyst. Remainder of cystic lining was completely enucleated with curettes and 4 molt and dropped in specimen cup to be taken for permanent pathology review. Site was copiously irrigated, no fluid return out of nares. bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:measurement:5 cc of DBX putty (bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:text:0, bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:text:1) placed in cyst. bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:measurement:6 cm x bone-graft-maxilla-anterior:measurement:7 cm Duragen collagen membrane placed over cyst. 3-0 vicryl was used to close vestibular incision site in a combination of interrupted and running fashion for watertight closure. Hemostasis was confirmed and the site was copiously irrigated. 

 

This concluded the surgical procedures.  The patient was turned over to the care of the anesthesia team. Pt  was extubated in the main operating room and transferred to the PACU with stable vital signs and spontaneous respirations having tolerated the procedure well without known surgical or anesthesia complications.

title: BSSO
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/04 Orthognathic/BSSO.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: BSSO

Findings: 

-bilateral mandibular sagittal osteotomy completed
-bilateral IAN released from canal and found to passive
-fixated with bilateral bsso:hardwareCount:0-hole bsso:text:0 dog bone plate with bsso:hardwareCount:1x monocortical screws
-closed w/ vicryl and chromic gut sutures
-class 2 posterior elastics

Technique:

The patient was met in the preoperative holding area. The plan for the surgery was reviewed with the patient. The history and physical examination was updated. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. 

The patient was then transported to the operating room and placed in the supine position on the operating room table. General anesthesia was induced with the anesthesia team via the intravenous route, and the airway was secured with a nasoendotracheal tube. A bracket and lug count was preformed. 

The eyes were protected with tegaderm, and pressure points were padded and checked. At this time, the oropharynx was suctioned free of debris and a moist throat pack was placed. The mandibular surgical sites were anesthetized with lidocaine 2% with 1:100,000 epinephrine with local infiltration as well as inferior alveolar and lingual nerve blocks. The patient was prepped and draped in sterile fashion for an orthognathic procedure. A surgical time-out was performed. 

Attention was directed to the right mandible, where bovie electrocautery was used to make a mucosal incision extending from the ascending ramus to the gingival cuff near the first premolar. The dissection was continued to the bony mandible along the ascending ramus and then along the lateral cortex of the right mandible. A #9 molt periosteal elevator was then used for subperiosteal dissection of the lateral body of the mandible to the anterior ramus, where the temporalis tendons were stripped using notched ramus retractor and a #9 periosteal elevator. The masseter and medial pterygoid muscles were stripped to allow placement of inferior border retractor. A subperiosteal plane was established on the medial border of the ascending ramus and the sigmoid notch and lingula were identified. The right inferior alveolar nerve was protected with a retractor. At this time, a reciprocating surgical handpiece with copious irrigation was used to make a medial horizontal osteotomy extending just beyond the lingula and then continued to the sagittal plane along the ascending ramus, and was carried monocortically through the superior aspect of the ramus to the lateral body of the mandible. The Piezo was then used to create an osteotomy at the inferior border and a monocortical osteotomy was carried obliquely to the medial ramus where it was joined with the second medial cut. The Piezo was then used to complete the inferior border osteotomy. The osteotomies were then recapitulated with a thin straight and then curved osteotome as indicated. A Smith spreader and an inferior border spreader were used serially and sequentially to complete the osteotomies; the mandible was then fractured on the right side. The nerve was gently dissected free from a small portion of the proximal segment after removing the overlying bony canal to allow passivity of the nerve, and then visualized completely intact within the distal mandibular segment. We completed the osteotomy and verified independent movement of the proximal and distal segments. Any proximal segment interferences were removed with a pineapple bur under copious irrigation with taking care to protect the IAN. 

Attention was then directed to the left mandible, where an identical incision, dissection and osteotomy were performed. After the split, the left inferior alveolar neurovascular bundle was gently dissected free from a small portion of the proximal segment, and then visualized completely intact within the distal mandibular segment. We then completed the osteotomy and verified independent movement of the proximal and distal segments. 

The patient was placed into maxillomandibular fixation using 24 gauge fish loop wires with the final splint. Patient was well-seated in the splint without interferences. A notched periosteal was used to reduce the proximal segments into the desired positions with a seated condyle by pushing superiorly, gently on the proximal segment at the gonial angle with the inferior border of proximal and distal segments aligned. We then noted passive fit of the segments. 

Attention was re-directed to the left mandible. A KLS bsso:hardwareCount:2-hole, bsso:text:1 thick, dog bone was then fixated using bsso:hardwareCount:3 bsso:measurement:0-mm and bsso:hardwareCount:4 bsso:measurement:1-mm monocortical screws to the proximal segment. Care was then taken to position the proximal segment superiorly into the glenoid fossa and to align the inferior border of the proximal segment with the distal segment using a notched instrument, where additional bsso:hardwareCount:5 bsso:measurement:2-mm monocortical screws were placed to fixate the plate to the distal segment. We then directed our attention to the right mandible, which was fixated in the same manner. 

Maxillomandibular fixation was then released and the occlusion was verified and noted to passively enter into the final splint in a reproducible and stable fashion. After copious sterile irrigation, the BSSO incisions were closed with interrupted 4-0 Vicryl and running locking 3-0 chromic gut sutures. 

At this time, the oropharynx was irrigated and suctioned free of debris, and the moist throat pack was removed. An orogastric tube was passed, decompressing the stomach. Occlusion was then rechecked and confirmed appropriately. Final bracket and lug count performed, accounting for all hardware. Patient was placed into bilateral class II guiding elastics. 10 cc of Exparel were injected at mandibular surgical sites via local infiltration. The face was cleaned with moist ray-tecs. The head wrap was removed and the nasoendotracheal tube supported and controlled at that time by the anesthesia team. Jaw bra was placed with gauze fluffs in the lower facial region and the lips liberally lubricated with hydrocortisone cream and bacitracin. The patient was turned over to the care of the anesthesia team, and was extubated in the main operating room and transferred to the PACU with appropriate vital signs and spontaneous respirations, having tolerated the procedures well without known surgical or anesthesia complications.

Attending: bsso:text:2 was scrubbed for the entirety of the case.

The head wrap was removed and the nasoendotracheal tube supported and controlled at that time by the anesthesia team. Jaw bra was placed with gauze fluffs in the lower facial region and the lips liberally lubricated with hydrocortisone cream and bacitracin. The patient was turned over to the care of the anesthesia team, and was extubated in the main operating room and transferred to the PACU with appropriate vital signs and spontaneous respirations, having tolerated the procedures well without known surgical or anesthesia complications.

Attending: [Attending] was scrubbed for the entirety of the case.

title: Discopexy with Mitek Anchors
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/05 TMJ/Discopexy with Mitek Anchors.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Discopexy with Mitek Anchors

 

Findings: Bilateral TMJ internal derangement with medially and anteriorly displaced articular discs, without perforation. Significant hyperemia and inflammation of the retrodiscal tissues noted bilaterally. Bilateral TMJs accessed via endaural incisions. Superior and inferior joint spaces explored, and adhesions removed. Inflamed retrodiscal tissues excised. Bilateral discopexies performed using Mitek anchors placed in the posterior-lateral condyles, securing the discs to the condyles. Condylar function and disc position verified bilaterally.

 

The patient was met in the pre-operative area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. The patient's ID and surgical site were verified. The patient was escorted to the operatory where he was placed in supine position on the operating room table. General anesthesia was induced via nasal rae and patient turned over to the surgical team where the time out was performed.   

 

Patient was prepped and draped in standard sterile fashion for the procedure and the left EAC protected with kittner sponge soaked with betadine. Ioban was placed over oral cavity to isolate the sterile surgical field. 

Attention was directed to the left TMJ. The endaural incision was marked and the incision site was infiltrated with 3 cc of 2% Lidocaine w/ 1:100,000 epinephrine. Using a #15, a left endaural incision was made over the previously marked site, down through dermis to subcutaneous tissue and continued anteriorly following the tragal cartilage. Blunt dissection continued above the zygomatic arch to identify the temporalis fascia, where a circumferential plane was created and connected to the avascular plane below the zygomatic arch. The zygomatic arch was identified, and Bovie electrocautery was used to create a horizontal incision through periosteum. A subperiosteal dissection was carried over the arch and lateral joint capsule visualized using blunt dissection with a #9 Molt elevator.

 

Once the lateral joint capsule was confirmed, 1 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 was used to insufflate the superior joint space. An incision was made between the lateral joint space and the glenoid fossa to enter the superior joint space. The superior joint space was explored with a Freer and adhesions from the meniscus to the glenoid fossa and articular eminence were removed. A #15 blade was then used to create an incision overlying the condylar head to identify the inferior joint space, which was then freed from the condyle and explored. No disc perforations were noted and the disc was medially and anteriorly displaced with significant hyperemia and inflammation of the retrodiscal tissue. A curved Freer was used to free the articular disc from the glenoid and condyle and repositioned gently over the condyle. Inflamed retrodiscal tissue was then excised.

 

At this time, a Mitek anchor was placed within the posterior, lateral aspect of the condyle. The attached braided suture was then secured to the medial and posterior aspects of the articular disc. The mandible was manipulated to confirm appropriate position of the disc with the condyle in function. The surgical site was copiously irrigated with sterile saline. Site hemostatic.

 

Attention directed towards closure. Deep 3-0 vicryl sutures were placed in a simple, interrupted fashion. 4-0 monocryl was used to close the deep dermal layer in a simple, interrupted fashion. Tension-free primary closure of the skin closed with a 5-0 prolene in a running, subcuticular suture. 

 

Attention was directed to the right TMJ. The endaural incision was marked and the incision site was infiltrated with 3 cc of 2% Lidocaine w/ 1:100,000 epinephrine. Using a #15, a right endaural incision was made over the previously marked site, down through dermis to subcutaneous tissue and continued anteriorly following the tragal cartilage. Blunt dissection continued above the zygomatic arch to identify the temporalis fascia, where a circumferential plane was created and connected to the avascular plane below the zygomatic arch. The zygomatic arch was identified, and Bovie electrocautery was used to create a horizontal incision through periosteum. A subperiosteal dissection was carried over the arch and lateral joint capsule visualized using blunt dissection with a #9 Molt elevator.

 

Once the lateral joint capsule was confirmed, 1 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 was used to insufflate the superior joint space. An incision was made between the lateral joint space and the glenoid fossa to enter the superior joint space. The superior joint space was explored with a Freer, and adhesions from the meniscus to the glenoid fossa and articular eminence were removed. A #15 blade was then used to create an incision overlying the condylar head to identify the inferior joint space, which was then freed from the condyle and explored. No disc perforations were noted and the disc was medially and anteriorly displaced. There was significant hyperemia and inflammation of the retrodiscal tissue noted. A curved Freer was used to free the articular disc from the glenoid and condyle and repositioned gently over the condyle. Inflamed retrodiscal tissue was then excised.

 

At this time, a Mitek anchor was placed within the posterior, lateral aspect of the condyle. The attached braided suture was then secured to the medial and posterior aspects of the articular disc. The mandible was manipulated to confirm appropriate position of the disc with the condyle in function. The surgical site was copiously irrigated with sterile saline. Site hemostatic.

 

Attention directed towards closure. Deep 3-0 vicryl sutures were placed in a simple, interrupted fashion. 4-0 monocryl was used to close the deep dermal layer in a simple, interrupted fashion. Tension-free primary closure of the skin closed with a 5-0 prolene in a running, subcuticular suture. 

 

This concluded the surgical procedure.  Patient turned over to anesthesia team where she was awoken from general anesthesia and extubated without incident.  The patient was transported to the recovery room with stable vital signs and spontaneous respirations.

title: Endobrow, Blepharoplasty Lowers, Canthopexy, LSR
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/06 Cosmetic & Facial/Endobrow, Blepharoplasty Lowers, Canthopexy, LSR.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Endobrow, Blepharoplasty Lowers, Canthopexy, LSR 

Findings: 

Tumescent administered in endoscopic brow dissection planes via Klein pump
R nasal labial and L nasal labial superficial red, slightly raised, firm skin lesions removed and sent for pathology
Endoscopic brow lift - median, 2x paramedian, and 2x temporal incisions made - dissected with use of endoscope. x2 0 Ethibond sutures used to secure temporal regions, endotines x2 used to secure paramedian regions - tissue allowed to stretch then re-draped on endotines at completion of case; all incisions within hairline closed with staples
Canthopexy - lateral brow incision made to lateral orbital rim. Wire passing bur used to create osteotomy. Lateral canthal tendon resuspended.
Lower blepharoplasty - transconjunctival incision made with bovie, fat repositioned, none excised. No sutures placed
CO2 ablative laser skin resurfacing completed with Deep (endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:0 mJ, endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:1s Repeat delay, endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:2 Hz) and Active (endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:3 mJ, endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:4 Hz, endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:5s Repeat delay) Fx handpieces

Technique: 

The patient was met in the pre-op holding area where they were identified by two patient identifiers, the patient's NPO status was confirmed, pre-operative HP was completed, all of the patient's questions were answered, the risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the procedure were reviewed and the consent was reviewed and signed, and the patient was marked for surgery. A peripheral IV was placed. The patient was transferred to the operating theater and placed supine on the operating table. The anesthetist induced the patient into general anesthesia and placed an oral endotracheal tube. The surgical team then secured the endotracheal tube. All pressure points were adequately padded and a time out was then performed with all staff members participating in the patient's care.

Corneal shields were placed with ophthalmic lubrication. The face was prepped with Betadine scrub. Tumescent solution mixture was prepared by combining 50cc 2% lidocaine, 10cc TXA, 1cc of 1:1,000 epinephrine in 500cc of 0.9% normal saline. This preparation was injected into the endoscopic brow dissection planes via Klein pump. The surgical sites were again prepped with Ophthalmic Betadine scrub and draped in the standard sterile fashion.

Melolabial fold skin lesion excision:
The bilateral melolabial fold skin lesions, identified as superficial red, slightly raised, firm lesions, were excised via elliptical sharp incisions with adequate margins. The right nasal labial and left nasal labial soft tissue specimens were sent for permanent pathologic evaluation. The sites were closed in layers with 4-0 monocryl deep dermal sutures followed by 6-0 plain gut interrupted sutures for skin closure. Hemostasis was confirmed.

Lower blepharoplasty with fat repositioning:
A Colorado Bovie tip was used to make a transconjunctival incision and expose the orbital fat pads bilaterally. The inferior orbital rim arcus marginalis was released with bovie and periosteal elevators and subperiosteal dissection was carried inferior to the orbital rim. The lateral and central orbital fat pads were identified and mobilized. Using 4-0 monocryl suture, the fat was repositioned by passing the suture from the lateral and central fat pads medially to exit through the skin of the melolabial fold near the ala. The suture was then passed back into the skin near the ala of the melolabial fold, creating a loop. A xeroform bolster dressing was placed under this loop of suture to provide additional support and prevent skin dimpling. The suture was then passed back laterally into the central and lateral fat pads and tied, securing the repositioned fat in its new position to address the tear trough deformity and provide midface volume restoration. This technique was repeated bilaterally with careful attention to symmetry. Hemostasis was confirmed. No excess orbital fat was excised and no skin excision was performed given the transconjunctival approach.

Endoscopic brow lift:
Lidocaine 2% with 1:100,000 epinephrine 4 mL was given to the supraorbital, lateral orbital and zygomatic regions. A marking pen was used to make 5 markings one inch posterior to the hairline. A vertical midline marking, two vertical paramedian markings and two transverse temporal markings within the Nasal Ala-Lateral Canthus vector each measuring about 3 cm in length. Incision was made full thickness from skin to bone with a 15 blade at the midline and paramedian regions. Dissection was carried deep to pericranium along the forehead, between the superior temporal lines, in a subperiosteal fashion using a #9 molt elevator and beavertail elevator. Using a downknuckle elevator, dissection was carried subperiosteally along the supraorbital rims elevating the periosteum off the rims and then along the lateral orbital rims. To dissect subperiosteally below the zygomaticofrontal suture to the inferior orbital rim a squid elevator was used. Careful attention was made along the medial supraorbital rims to stop 2 cm superior to the rim and avoid damage to the supraorbital and supratrochlear nerves.

A 30 degree rigid endoscope was then inserted in the midline incision and advanced to the supraorbital rim region. A down knuckle elevator was placed in the paramedian regions to dissect subperiosteally around the supratrochlear and supraorbital nerves. Using the upknuckle elevator placed through the paramedian incision, the periosteum underneath the brows was scored and separated bilaterally, making sure to minimize any medial dissection and continued until 1 cm of periosteum was elevated from the orbital rim.

Using a 15 blade, incisions were made through skin at the temporal markings and dissection was made through the temporoparietal fascia down to the temporalis fascia. Bleeding was controlled using the Bovie. Once the temporalis fascia was identified, a #9 molt elevator and beavertail elevator were used to dissect along the temporalis fascial plane laterally, posteriorly and anteriorly. The dissection was then carried medially through the superior temporal lines to connect the dissections between the subperiosteal forehead and temporalis fascial plane. Using the beavertail elevator, careful subperiosteal dissection was continued along the zygoma and lateral orbit until tissues were adequately released.

Canthopexy:
Attention was drawn to the lateral upper eyelids. 6 cc of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was injected. A lateral upper eyelid incision approximately 1 cm in length was made to approach the lateral orbital rim. The superior lateral orbital rim was identified through this approach. While protecting the orbital contents, a wire passing bur was used to create an osteotomy through the orbital rim. The lateral canthal tendon was identified and canthopexy was performed using 3-0 Prolene suture in a superior vector. Good lift was noted and the procedure was repeated on the contralateral side with symmetry noted. The sites were closed with 6-0 plain gut to approximate the orbicularis oculi followed by running 6-0 plain gut for skin closure.

Fixation of Brow:
Temporal fixation was completed using 2-0 Ethibond sutures through the temporoparietal fascia and affixed to the temporalis fascia in a vector that followed the Nasal Ala-Lateral Canthus line. One suture was passed and tied while the assistant superiorly elevated the lateral forehead with digital pressure. The procedure was then repeated on the contralateral side and assessed for symmetry. Next, osteotomies for the Endotines were prepared at paramedian incisions and Endotines placed. The brows were elevated and tissues supported by the Endotines. The tissues were allowed to stretch and then re-draped on the Endotines at completion of the case. The temporal incisions were then assessed for bleeding and noted to be hemostatic. All incisions within the hairline were closed with staples. Adequate brow lift was appreciated with good symmetry.

Full-face CO2 Laser Resurfacing:
External eye shields were worn by the patient during the entire procedure, and wavelength specific eye protection was worn by all staff in the room. Mirrors were stowed, and opaque film applied to the windows. The vacuum was used for smoke evacuation, and laser masks were worn by all staff in the room. Fire extinguishers were on standby, and a wet gauze/towel was on the field.

Laser: Lumenis UltraPulse CO2, 10,600nm
Handpiece: DeepFX
Treatment area: Full face
Energy: endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:6 mJ
Rate: endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:7 Hz
Repeat delay: endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:8 seconds

The DeepFX handpiece was used to deliver ablative fractional treatment to the entire face with pulses delivered in rows with minimal overlap.

Laser: Lumenis UltraPulse CO2, 10,600nm
Handpiece: ActiveFX
Treatment area: Full face
Energy: endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:9 mJ
Rate: endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:10 Hz
Repeat delay: endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:measurement:11 seconds

The ActiveFX handpiece was then used to deliver a second pass of treatment to the entire face, again with pulses delivered in rows with minimal overlap. Hemostasis was confirmed following laser treatment.

Corneal shields were removed and the eyes rinsed with balanced salt solution. The hair was cleansed with shampoo and normal saline. Erythromycin ophthalmic ointment was applied to the periocular areas. Post-laser skincare protocols were initiated with appropriate wound care instructions provided. The patient was then turned over to the anesthesia service who awoke the patient, extubated them, and transported them to postop care where they recovered uneventfully. Upon meeting discharge criteria the patient was released under the care of their escort with appropriate follow up.

endobrow-blepharoplasty-lowers-canthopexy-lsr:text:0 was scrubbed in for the entirety of the case.

One suture was passed and tied while the assistant superiorly elevated the lateral forehead with digital pressure. The procedure was then repeated on the contralateral side and assessed for symmetry. Next, osteotomies for the Endotines were prepared at paramedian incisions and Endotines placed. The brows were elevated and tissues supported by the Endotines. The tissues were allowed to stretch and then re-draped on the Endotines at completion of the case. The temporal incisions were then assessed for bleeding and noted to be hemostatic. All incisions within the hairline were closed with staples. Adequate brow lift was appreciated with good symmetry.

Full-face CO2 Laser Resurfacing:
External eye shields were worn by the patient during the entire procedure, and wavelength specific eye protection was worn by all staff in the room. Mirrors were stowed, and opaque film applied to the windows. The vacuum was used for smoke evacuation, and laser masks were worn by all staff in the room. Fire extinguishers were on standby, and a wet gauze/towel was on the field.

Laser: Lumenis UltraPulse CO2, 10,600nm
Handpiece: DeepFX
Treatment area: Full face
Energy: [X] mJ
Rate: [X] Hz
Repeat delay: [X] seconds

The DeepFX handpiece was used to deliver ablative fractional treatment to the entire face with pulses delivered in rows with minimal overlap.

Laser: Lumenis UltraPulse CO2, 10,600nm
Handpiece: ActiveFX
Treatment area: Full face
Energy: [X] mJ
Rate: [X] Hz
Repeat delay: [X] seconds

The ActiveFX handpiece was then used to deliver a second pass of treatment to the entire face, again with pulses delivered in rows with minimal overlap. Hemostasis was confirmed following laser treatment.

Corneal shields were removed and the eyes rinsed with balanced salt solution. The hair was cleansed with shampoo and normal saline. Erythromycin ophthalmic ointment was applied to the periocular areas. Post-laser skincare protocols were initiated with appropriate wound care instructions provided. The patient was then turned over to the anesthesia service who awoke the patient, extubated them, and transported them to postop care where they recovered uneventfully. Upon meeting discharge criteria the patient was released under the care of their escort with appropriate follow up.

[Attending] was scrubbed in for the entirety of the case.

title: Enucleation and Curettage of Cyst, Right Mandible
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/03 Pathology, Salivary & Infection/Excision Cyst Right Mandible.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: Enucleation and Curettage of Cyst, Right Mandible



Findings: excision-cyst-right-mandible:side:0 excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:0 cystic lesion at [site/tooth #] measuring approximately excision-cyst-right-mandible:measurement:0 cm, excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:1, associated with [impacted tooth #__ / no tooth]. excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:2 nerve excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:3. Lesion enucleated in excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:4 and submitted for permanent pathology. Bony cavity curetted; excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:5. [Tooth #__ extracted / no extraction]. No pathologic fracture. Hemostasis achieved.

The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed, including bleeding, infection, nerve injury (excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:6) with potential paresthesia, damage to adjacent teeth, pathologic fracture, recurrence, and need for further surgery. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. The patient was escorted to the operating room and placed in supine position on the operating table. Anesthesia monitors were placed. The patient was placed under general anesthesia and intubated via a excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:7 endotracheal route without incident. The oral cavity was suctioned and a throat pack was placed and the mouth was rinsed with peridex. The patient was then prepped and draped for surgery in the usual sterile fashion.

Attention was directed to the excision-cyst-right-mandible:side:1 excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:8 excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:9 where the site was anesthetized with excision-cyst-right-mandible:measurement:1 cc of excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:10 lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine [and an inferior alveolar/mental nerve block was performed]. Using a #15 blade, a excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:11 incision was made from [tooth/region __ to __] with excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:12 as needed. A full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap was elevated and a subperiosteal dissection completed, exposing the underlying cortex and any bony expansion or dehiscence over the lesion. The excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:13 nerve was excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:14.

[If aspiration performed: An 18-gauge needle was used to aspirate the lesion prior to entry, returning [straw-colored fluid / nothing / keratinaceous material].] A bony window was developed over the lesion with a excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:15 bur under copious irrigation, and any thinned overlying cortex was removed. The cystic lining was carefully separated from the bony walls with periosteal elevators and curettes and enucleated in excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:16. The specimen was placed in formalin and submitted for permanent pathologic examination. [Tooth #__ was extracted atraumatically and the follicle removed with the specimen.]

The residual bony cavity was thoroughly curetted and inspected; [a peripheral ostectomy was performed with a round bur under irrigation / Carnoy's solution was applied for [X] minutes with adjacent structures protected, then copiously irrigated / no adjunctive treatment was performed]. Hemostasis was confirmed. [Cavity packed with [collagen/DBM/gelfoam] / left to fill with clot / grafted as noted.] The site was copiously irrigated with sterile saline.

The flap was repositioned and the incision closed with excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:17 [Vicryl/chromic gut] in a excision-cyst-right-mandible:enumText:18 fashion, taking care to re-suspend the papillae. The oral cavity was irrigated, suctioned, and the throat pack was removed. All counts were correct.

This concluded the surgical procedure. Care of the patient was then turned over to the anesthesia team. The patient was extubated in the operating room without incident and transferred to the recovery room with stable vital signs and spontaneous respirations verified.

Specimen: [cyst lining ± tooth #__] to permanent pathology.

PROCEDURE: Enucleation and Curettage of Cyst, Right Mandible

[TEMPLATE NOTE — 2026-06-14: The original "Excision Cyst Right Mandible" body in OP Notes.docx was a mis-paste: it contained a right ZMC/zygomaticomaxillary buttress ORIF (Rowe disimpaction, Synthes L-plate), not any cyst procedure. That fracture text is preserved in _archive/OP Notes - original extracted.txt. The body below is a newly drafted generic enucleation/curettage skeleton in the surgeon's voice — verify every step against the actual case before signing, and add Carnoy's/peripheral ostectomy, marsupialization, or resection language as the lesion dictates.]

Findings: [R/L] [mandible/maxilla] cystic lesion at [site/tooth #] measuring approximately [X] cm, [well-corticated/expansile], associated with [impacted tooth #__ / no tooth]. [Inferior alveolar / mental / lingual / infraorbital] nerve [visualized and protected / not encountered]. Lesion enucleated in [toto / piecemeal] and submitted for permanent pathology. Bony cavity curetted; [peripheral ostectomy with round bur / Carnoy's solution applied / not applied]. [Tooth #__ extracted / no extraction]. No pathologic fracture. Hemostasis achieved.

The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed, including bleeding, infection, nerve injury ([IAN/mental/lingual]) with potential paresthesia, damage to adjacent teeth, pathologic fracture, recurrence, and need for further surgery. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. The patient was escorted to the operating room and placed in supine position on the operating table. Anesthesia monitors were placed. The patient was placed under general anesthesia and intubated via a [nasal/oral] endotracheal route without incident. The oral cavity was suctioned and a throat pack was placed and the mouth was rinsed with peridex. The patient was then prepped and draped for surgery in the usual sterile fashion.

Attention was directed to the [R/L] [mandibular/maxillary] [vestibule/alveolus] where the site was anesthetized with [X] cc of [1%/2%] lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine [and an inferior alveolar/mental nerve block was performed]. Using a #15 blade, a [crestal/sulcular/vestibular] incision was made from [tooth/region __ to __] with [distal release / hockey-stick extension] as needed. A full-thickness mucoperiosteal flap was elevated and a subperiosteal dissection completed, exposing the underlying cortex and any bony expansion or dehiscence over the lesion. The [IAN/mental/lingual/infraorbital] nerve was [identified and protected / not encountered].

[If aspiration performed: An 18-gauge needle was used to aspirate the lesion prior to entry, returning [straw-colored fluid / nothing / keratinaceous material].] A bony window was developed over the lesion with a [701/702] bur under copious irrigation, and any thinned overlying cortex was removed. The cystic lining was carefully separated from the bony walls with periosteal elevators and curettes and enucleated in [toto / piecemeal]. The specimen was placed in formalin and submitted for permanent pathologic examination. [Tooth #__ was extracted atraumatically and the follicle removed with the specimen.]

The residual bony cavity was thoroughly curetted and inspected; [a peripheral ostectomy was performed with a round bur under irrigation / Carnoy's solution was applied for [X] minutes with adjacent structures protected, then copiously irrigated / no adjunctive treatment was performed]. Hemostasis was confirmed. [Cavity packed with [collagen/DBM/gelfoam] / left to fill with clot / grafted as noted.] The site was copiously irrigated with sterile saline.

The flap was repositioned and the incision closed with [3-0/4-0] [Vicryl/chromic gut] in a [running/interrupted] fashion, taking care to re-suspend the papillae. The oral cavity was irrigated, suctioned, and the throat pack was removed. All counts were correct.

This concluded the surgical procedure. Care of the patient was then turned over to the anesthesia team. The patient was extubated in the operating room without incident and transferred to the recovery room with stable vital signs and spontaneous respirations verified.
