Attention was directed to implant site [#__], the Nobel active system was followed to develop a [diameter]x[length]mm dimension osteotomy. Given the position of the mental nerve this implant was placed at an angle. A [diameter]x[length]mm implant placed and torqued to greater than [X] Ncm. A [X] degree MUA was placed and checked to ensure implant profile lined up to palatal cusps of upper posterior teeth. 
Verification of abutment seating was completed with multiple periapical radiographs at each implant. 

Closure
The mandibular surgical sites were irrigated with copious amounts of sterile saline. Healing caps were placed on each abutment. Midline was tacked with an interrupted 3-0 Chromic gut suture. Next the crestal incision was closed with 3-0 Chromic gut suture in a running fashion. 


At this time, the oropharynx was irrigated and suctioned free of debris. An orogastric tube was passed, decompressing the stomach. 10cc of exparel was injected at maxillary and mandibular surgical sites via local infiltration. The face was cleaned with moist ray-tecs. The head wrap was removed and the patient was turned over to the anesthesia team. The lips were liberally lubricated with hydrocortisone cream. The patient was extubated in the main operating room and transferred to the PACU with appropriate vital signs and spontaneous respirations, having tolerated the procedures well.

[Attending(s)] were present for the entirety of the procedure.

title: TJR with Custom Stryker TMJ Concepts
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/05 TMJ/TJR with Custom Stryker TMJ Concepts.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: TJR with Custom Stryker TMJ Concepts

 

FINDINGS: Endaural and submandibular incisions performed bilaterally. Stryker custom fossa and ramal cut and fixation guides placed. Condylectomy and discectomy performed bilaterally. Stryker custom fossa components placed, secured with tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:0 monocortical screws. Stryker custom condylar prostheses placed, secured with tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:1 bicortical screws. Floseal inserted in bilateral incisions. Closed with 3-0 Vicryl deeps, 4-0 Monocryl subcutaneous, and 5-0 Prolene running subcuticular. Occlusion stable, repeatable and coincident with pre-op plan. tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:2 IMF screws remain in place for guiding elastics.

 

TECHNIQUE:

The patient was greeted in the main operating room holding area where identification was verified, health history updated, and any questions were answered to apparent satisfaction.  Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered. The surgical site was verified and the patient expressed desires to proceed as planned. Anesthesia evaluated and deemed the patient appropriate to proceed, then transported the patient to the operating room via gurney.

 

The patient was placed on the operating room table in the supine position, secured with a security strap, and sequential compression devices were placed on the calves and pressure points padded. General anesthesia was induced, and a successful nasal-endotracheal intubation was achieved. The tube was secured to the septum with 2-0 silk. Hair near the bilateral TMJ surgical area was isolated with tape. A head wrap consisting of a blue towel and silk tape was used to protect the patient's scalp and forehead in addition to providing anchorage to secure the endotracheal tube. The eyes were lubricated with ophthalmic bacitracin then secured closed with Tegaderms. Otoscopic examination revealed bilateral tympanic membranes were intact. The posterior oropharynx was suctioned and a moistened Ray-Tec throat pack was placed. The mouth was disinfected with 0.12% chlorhexidine gluconate mouth rinse. A surgical timeout was performed. Local anesthesia in the form of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi was injected to the bilateral maxilla and mandible as local infiltrates and tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:3 total tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:measurement:0-mm IMF screws (tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:4 in the maxilla and tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:5 in the mandible) were inserted in standard fashion, taking care to avoid the roots of nearby teeth. The bilateral ear canals were treated with a few drops of ophthalmic betadine.

 

The patient's head and neck, including the ETT, and headwrap were prepped with betadine paint. The patient was then draped in sterile fashion for an open TMJ procedure, with an Ioban dressing placed over the patient's mouth to ensure maintenance of a sterile field. Both external auditory canals (EACs) were irrigated with Vancomycin antibiotic solution again. All TMJ surgical sites were irrigated with Vancomycin solution throughout the procedure.

 

Right Endaural and Submandibular Incisions, Condylectomy, Discectomy, Guides Placement

Attention was then directed to the right TMJ procedure, where a marked endsural incision was infiltrated with 2% lidocaine w/ 1:100,000 epinephrine local anesthesia. A #15 blade was then used to make an endaural incision from the earlobe to the root of the helix. Sharp dissection was done anterior to the tragal cartilage in the supraperichondrial plane down to the root of the zygomatic arch. At the superior aspect of the incision, blunt dissection continued until the superficial layer of the temporalis fascia was identified and the regional tissue undermined. The periosteum overlying the zygomatic arch was incised, and subperiosteal dissection continued to the anterior aspect of the zygomatic arch. Blunt dissection continued inferiorly over the lateral capsule. A vertical incision was made over the capsule and dissection into the superior and inferior joint spaces isolated the TMJ disc.

 

Attention then directed towards the initial osteotomy of the condyle. With the condyle seated as superiorly as possible, a Sonopet was used to create an initial osteotomy with condylar retractors seated at the medial border of the condyle. The osteotomy was then completed with a T-handle osteotome, and the superior portion of the condyle was removed after the lateral pterygoid was dissected. Hemostasis was obtained with bipolar electrocautery. Surgicel was placed for additional hemostasis. The site was packed with Ray-Tec gauze.

 

Attention was then directed on the right submandibular incision. A surgical marking pen was used to delineate the proposed curvilinear incision, inferior to the angle of the mandible, 2 cm away from the posterior/inferior border of the mandible. 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine was administered for hemostasis and local anesthetic. A #15 blade was used to incise through skin and subcutaneous tissue, down to the subcutaneous fat. Curved Metzenbaum scissors were used to undermine the subcutaneous tissue superiorly to the border of the mandible. Bovie electrocautery was then used to dissect through fat down to the platysma muscle. The platysma was then tented upwards and incised with sharp dissection, with the utilization of a facial nerve stimulator. The posterior border of the mandible was identified and the pterygomasseteric sling was incised with Bovie electrocautery. Subperiosteal dissection continued on the lateral aspect of the mandible inferiorly and posteriorly to the border as well as superiorly towards the joint space until both spaces were continuous. The custom cut guide was inserted inferiorly, ensuring complete freedom from soft tissue entrapment, and the guide was secured to the lateral ramus with tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:6 tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:measurement:1-mm screws drilled in the predictive holes.

 

From the endaural surgical site, condylar retractors were again placed to isolate the condyle. A second osteotomy cut was created with the Sonopet along the custom cut guide. The remaining portion of the right condylectomy was completed with a T-handle osteotomy and the remainder of the condyle was removed. The remainder of the TMJ disc and peri-discal tissue was then carefully excised, and the glenoid fossa and articular eminence were relieved of tissue utilizing curved Freers and Molt elevators.

 

Attention was then directed towards placement of the right custom condylar fixation guide. The guide was placed within the incision and on the fossa ensuring no soft tissue entrapment was present. After confirmation of complete seating, the guide was fixated with tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:7 tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:measurement:2-mm screws in the predictive holes.

 

The right face surgical sites were packed with damp Vancomycin solution-soaked gauze, one in each incision site. The skin, surrounding area, and external canal were again irrigated with Vancomycin solution. The sites were then covered with Tegaderms to ensure isolation.

 

Left endaural and submandibular incisions, condylectomy, discectomy, guides placement

A similar approach was taken to expose the left condyle, fossa, and lateral ramus via endaural incision and submandibular incision, condylectomy, and placement of the fossa and condylar cut and predictive drilling guides.Following completion of drilling the predictive holes for the prostheses bilaterally, the guides were removed.

 

Placement of fossa and condylar prostheses

Attention then directed towards placement of the right Stryker fossa component, which was irrigated with vancomycin antibiotic solution. The fossa was placed within the endaural incision and the fixation holes aligned with the predictive holes, with good stability of the fossa component. The fossa component was then secured with tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:8 tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:measurement:3-mm screws. Attention then directed towards placement of the right condylar component, which was placed through the submandibular incision. The fixation holes were then aligned with the pre-drilled predictive holes, and with the condyle completely seated within the fossa component, the condylar prosthesis was fixated with tjr-with-custom-stryker-tmj-concepts:hardwareCount:9 bicortical screws. The similar procedure was then performed on the left. Surgical sites were packed with moist raytec.

 

Occlusion check

Sterile blue towels and a drape were placed over bilateral TMJ surgical areas. Attention was then directed intraorally. The patient's occlusion was noted to be stable, reproducible, and coincident with the pre-operative planned occlusion. The top drapes and blue towels were removed, and the surgical team donned new gloves and gowns. The mouth was re-prepped with betadine, and a new Ioban dressing was placed over the mouth to re-establish the sterile field.

 

All remaining TMJ Concepts fossa and condylar screws were then drilled and placed under copious vancomycin irrigation with the pre-planned screw lengths.

All remaining TMJ concepts fossa and condylar screws were then drilled and placed under copious vancomycin irrigation with the pre-planned screw lengths.

 

Incision closure

Attention directed towards closure of the bilateral incisions. All surgical sites were copiously irrigated with vancomycin solution. The submandibular incisions were closed with 3-0 vicryls for the pterygomasseteric sling and deep layer, 4-0 Monocryl for the dermal layer, and 5-0 Prolene running subcuticulars. Floseal was placed through the endaural incisions, which were then closed with 3-0 deep Vicryl sutures to create a watertight plane, 4-0 Monocryl for deep dermals, and 5-0 Prolene running subcuticular sutures. The incisions were then dressed with Dermabond and Steri-Strips.

 

Occlusion was again verified to be stable, repeatable, and coincident with the pre-operative plan.

 

The oral cavity and posterior oropharynx were thoroughly suctioned with a Yankauer suction tip. Throat pack was removed. Guiding elastics placed. An orogastric tube was passed to empty the stomach. Bacitracin was placed to all extra-oral incisions. The patient's face was cleansed with moistened lap pads and the head wrap dressing with associated sutures and tape was removed. A JawBra dressing was placed. The eyes were rinsed with balanced salt solution. The patient emerged from general anesthesia, and was extubated in the operating room. The patient tolerated the surgery and the anesthesia well and was transported to the Post-Anesthesia Care Unit where the patient maintained normal stable vital signs. There were no procedural or anesthetic complications. This concluded the surgical procedures.

title: TJR with Stock Biomet
sourcePath: Note Writing/Op Note Templates/05 TMJ/TJR with Stock Biomet.md
bodyTemplate:
PROCEDURE: TJR with Stock Biomet

 

FINDINGS:  Bilateral endaural and retromandibular incisions. Nerve checkpoint utilized throughout surgery to identify and avoid facial nerve throughout the case. TJR completed with Biomet stock prostheses. Custom guides used for lateral ramus reduction, fossa recontouring, and fixation screws. MMF completed with IMF screws. Right TMJ: Biomet tjr-with-stock-biomet:text:0 fossa, Narrow tjr-with-stock-biomet:text:1 condylar component secured with tjr-with-stock-biomet:hardwareCount:0 tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:0-mm screws. Left TMJ: Biomet tjr-with-stock-biomet:text:2 fossa fixated with tjr-with-stock-biomet:hardwareCount:1 tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:1-mm monocortical screws; Narrow tjr-with-stock-biomet:text:3 condylar component secured with tjr-with-stock-biomet:hardwareCount:2 tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:2-mm screws. Abdominal fat harvested and placed around bilateral prostheses. Occlusion verified throughout procedure. Specimen(s) 1. R condyle  disc

 

TECHNIQUE: TJR with Stock Biomet

 

The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered.

The patient was placed on the operating room table in a supine position and secured with security strap. Sequential compression devices were placed on her calves and pressure points were padded. General anesthesia was induced and a successful nasal-endotracheal intubation was achieved. Hair near TMJ surgical areas was isolated with tape. A head wrap consisting of a blue towel and silk tape was used to protect the patient’s scalp and forehead in addition to providing anchorage to secure the endotracheal tube. The eyes were lubricated with ophthalmic ointment then secured closed with tegaderm. Otoscopic examination revealed right tympanic membranes were intact. A time out was performed that included verification of the patient’s identity, using two unique identifiers and the intended surgical site.

 

The posterior oropharynx was suctioned and a moistened throat pack was placed. The mouth was disinfected with 0.12% chlorhexidine gluconate mouth rinse. tjr-with-stock-biomet:hardwareCount:3 tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:3-mm IMF screws were placed in the mandible, and tjr-with-stock-biomet:hardwareCount:4 tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:4-mm IMF screws were placed in the maxilla in an evenly distributed manner.

 

The patient’s abdomen was then prepped with chlorhexidine skin prep and isolated from the surgical field. The patient’s head and neck were then prepped with betadine. Ioban dressing was placed over the patient’s mouth to insure a sterile field. Left endaural and retromandibular surgical sites were anesthetized with 5mL of 1% Lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi via local infiltration.

 

Next, attention was directed to the left endaural region where a #15 blade was used to make an incision from her left ear lobe to the root of the helix. Sharp dissection was performed anterior to the tragal cartilage in the supraperichondrial plane down to the zygomatic arch. At the superior aspect of the incision, blunt dissection continued until the superficial layer of the temporalis was identified and the regional tissue undermined. Hemostasis was obtained with electrocautery. The periosteum overlying the zygomatic arch was incised and subperiosteal dissection continued to the anterior aspect of the zygomatic arch. An incision was made on the lateral joint capsule to access the joint in a T pattern. The condylar head was visualized. The lateral condyle and ramus were bluntly dissected in a subperiosteal plane. A moist Ray-Tec soaked in tranexamic acid was compressed into the left preauricular surgical field.

 

Attention was turned to the left neck, where a retromandibular incision was performed. A surgical marking pen was used to delineate the proposed surgical site 1 cm below the left ear lobe curvilinear to the gonial angle, about 3 cm long. A #15 blade was used to dissect through skin and platysma, then Metzenbaum scissors were used for blunt dissection down to the angle of the mandible, deep to the parotid gland. The marginal mandibular branch of the facial nerve was protected superiorly with a malleable retractor. A portable nerve stimulator was set at 0.5 mA and used throughout the dissection. The posterior border of the mandible was identified and the pterygomasseteric sling was incised with Bovie electrocautery. A subperiosteal dissection continued on the lateral aspect of the mandible superior toward the joint space until both preauricular and retromandibular spaces were continuous.

 

Attention was directed back to the left endaural surgical site. Condylar retractors were placed anterior and posterior to the condyle. The condylar cutting guide was placed and tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:5 mm of condylar height was removed using the Piezo as planned. The osteotomy was completed with a T-handle osteotome and the left mandibular condyle was removed. The temporomandibular disc was separated from the lateral pterygoid muscle and retrodiscal tissue with Bovie electrocautery, which was then used to achieve hemostasis. The condyle and articular disc were submitted for histological analysis. Next, a separate lateral ramus reduction guide was placed and the planned osseous recontouring was performed with a power rasp with copious irrigation to achieve a flat, even lateral surface for the prosthesis. Next, separate fossa reduction and drill guides were used to drill pre-determined holes for the fossa component and for fossa recontouring. A Piezo and power rasp with copious irrigation were used to shape the articular eminence for adequate fit of the fossa component. The left lateral ramus was contoured to fit the left condylar component with a power rasp and copious irrigation. Next, a Biomet stock tjr-with-stock-biomet:text:4 fossa component was placed at the left TMJ fossa and a stable fit was confirmed with matching of the pre-drilled holes. It was then secured with tjr-with-stock-biomet:hardwareCount:5 tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:6-mm screws. The surgical sites were irrigated and a vancomycin-soaked Ray-Tec was compressed in the incisions.

A Piezo and power rasp with copious irrigation were used to shape the articular eminence for adequate fit of the fossa component. Next, a Biomet stock tjr-with-stock-biomet:text:5 fossa component was placed at the right TMJ fossa and a stable fit was confirmed with matching of the pre-drilled holes. Attention then directed towards the left lateral ramus. A left Biomet tjr-with-stock-biomet:text:7 narrow condylar component was then placed through the left neck incision and seated into the fossa component, which was secured with tjr-with-stock-biomet:hardwareCount:8 tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:10-mm bicortical screws while the mandible was completely seated in the appropriate position of the fossa.

 

Towels and drapes were placed over the bilateral TMJ surgical sites to separate the sterile filed from the mouth. The oral cavity was entered through the Ioban ensuring no cross contamination with sterile field. Maxillomandibular fixation was released and the patient's occlusion was stable and was in accordance to her pre-operative occlusion. The patient was then re-prepped and draped in sterile fashion for abdominal fat harvest and fat transfer to the surgical sites and closure.

 

Attention was directed to patient's abdomen. Utilizing a 15 blade a curvilinear incision was made in the infraumbilical region. Sharp dissection continued to the subcutaneous fat. Dermabond was applied at the end of closure.

 

tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:12 cc of abdominal fat was then placed through the right endaural incision around the prosthesis. Copious irrigation with vancomycin solution was used in the right surgical sites. The pterygomasseteric sling was re-approximated with 3-0 vicryls in a simple interrupted fashion. The platysma muscle was approximated with 3-0 vicryl sutures. Deep dermal sutures using 4-0 Monocryl were also placed in a buried interrupted fashion. The skin was closed superficially with 5-0 plain gut in a simple, running fashion. The right endaural incision was then closed with 3-0 deep vicryls for the muscular layer. Deep dermal sutures using 4-0 Monocryl were also placed in a buried fashion. The skin was closed superficially with 5-0 plain gut in a simple, running fashion. The incisions were dressed with Dermabond.

 

tjr-with-stock-biomet:measurement:13 cc of abdominal fat was then placed through the left endaural incision around the prosthesis. Copious irrigation with vancomycin solution was used in the left surgical sites. The pterygomasseteric sling was re-approximated with 3-0 vicryls in a simple interrupted fashion. The platysma muscle was approximated with 3-0 vicryl sutures. Deep dermal sutures using 4-0 Monocryl were also placed in a buried interrupted fashion. The skin was closed superficially with 5-0 plain gut in a simple, running fashion. The right endaural incision was then closed with 3-0 deep vicryls for the muscular layer. Deep dermal sutures using 4-0 Monocryl were also placed in a buried fashion. The skin was closed superficially with 5-0 plain gut in a simple, running fashion. The incisions were dressed with Dermabond.

 

The Ioban dressing was removed, the occlusion re-verified. The throat pack was removed. A JawBra and fluffs were placed for compression on the patient's surgical sites. The Tegaderms were removed from the eyes and rinsed with BSS. The head wrap was removed and the patient was turned over to the anesthesia team. The general anesthesia was reversed and the patient was extubated and nasogastric tube removed and taken to the PACU in stable condition, maintaining stable vital signs.

rawBody:
PROCEDURE: TJR with Stock Biomet

 

FINDINGS:  Bilateral endaural and retromandibular incisions. Nerve checkpoint utilized throughout surgery to identify and avoid facial nerve throughout the case. TJR completed with Biomet stock prostheses. Custom guides used for lateral ramus reduction, fossa recontouring, and fixation screws. MMF completed with IMF screws. Right TMJ: Biomet [size] fossa, Narrow [size] condylar component secured with [#] [length]-mm screws. Left TMJ: Biomet [size] fossa fixated with [#] [length]-mm monocortical screws; Narrow [size] condylar component secured with [#] [length]-mm screws. Abdominal fat harvested and placed around bilateral prostheses. Occlusion verified throughout procedure. Specimen(s) 1. R condyle  disc

 

TECHNIQUE: TJR with Stock Biomet

 

The patient was identified in the preoperative holding area. Risks, benefits, alternatives, and complications of the surgery were reviewed with the patient. Consent was verified. All questions were answered.

The patient was placed on the operating room table in a supine position and secured with security strap. Sequential compression devices were placed on her calves and pressure points were padded. General anesthesia was induced and a successful nasal-endotracheal intubation was achieved. Hair near TMJ surgical areas was isolated with tape. A head wrap consisting of a blue towel and silk tape was used to protect the patient’s scalp and forehead in addition to providing anchorage to secure the endotracheal tube. The eyes were lubricated with ophthalmic ointment then secured closed with tegaderm. Otoscopic examination revealed right tympanic membranes were intact. A time out was performed that included verification of the patient’s identity, using two unique identifiers and the intended surgical site.

 

The posterior oropharynx was suctioned and a moistened throat pack was placed. The mouth was disinfected with 0.12% chlorhexidine gluconate mouth rinse. [#] [length]-mm IMF screws were placed in the mandible, and [#] [length]-mm IMF screws were placed in the maxilla in an evenly distributed manner.

 

The patient’s abdomen was then prepped with chlorhexidine skin prep and isolated from the surgical field. The patient’s head and neck were then prepped with betadine. Ioban dressing was placed over the patient’s mouth to insure a sterile field. Left endaural and retromandibular surgical sites were anesthetized with 5mL of 1% Lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi via local infiltration.

A moist Ray-Tec soaked in tranexamic acid was compressed into the left preauricular surgical field.

 

Attention was turned to the left neck, where a retromandibular incision was performed. A surgical marking pen was used to delineate the proposed surgical site 1 cm below the left ear lobe curvilinear to the gonial angle, about 3 cm long. The posterior border of the mandible was identified and the pterygomasseteric sling was incised with Bovie electrocautery. A subperiosteal dissection continued on the lateral aspect of the mandible superior toward the joint space until both preauricular and retromandibular spaces were continuous.

 

Attention was directed back to the left endaural surgical site. Condylar retractors were placed anterior and posterior to the condyle. The condylar cutting guide was placed and [X] mm of condylar height was removed using the Piezo as planned. The osteotomy was completed with a T-handle osteotome and the left mandibular condyle was removed. The temporomandibular disc was separated from the lateral pterygoid muscle and retrodiscal tissue with Bovie electrocautery, which was then used to achieve hemostasis. The condyle and articular disc were submitted for histological analysis. Next, separate fossa reduction and drill guides were used to drill pre-determined holes for the fossa component and for fossa recontouring. A Piezo and power rasp with copious irrigation were used to shape the articular eminence for adequate fit of the fossa component. The left lateral ramus was contoured to fit the left condylar component with a power rasp and copious irrigation. Next, a Biomet stock [size] fossa component was placed at the left TMJ fossa and a stable fit was confirmed with matching of the pre-drilled holes. It was then secured with [#] [length]-mm screws. The surgical sites were irrigated and a vancomycin-soaked Ray-Tec was compressed in the incisions.

 

Next, attention was directed to the right endaural and retromandibular incisions where 5 ml of 2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epi was injected. Attention was then directed to the right lateral ramus where a right Biomet [size] narrow condylar component was then placed through the right neck incision and seated into the fossa component, which was secured with [#] [length]-mm bicortical screws while the mandible was completely seated in the appropriate position of the fossa.

 

Attention then directed towards the left lateral ramus. A left Biomet [size] narrow condylar component was then placed through the left neck incision and seated into the fossa component, which was secured with [#] [length]-mm bicortical screws while the mandible was completely seated in the appropriate position of the fossa.

The abdominal surgical site was closed in layers with deep 3-0 vicryl sutures and superficial closure achieved with 4-0 monocryl sutures subcutaneous sutures and 5-0 plain gut skin sutures. Dermabond was applied at the end of closure.

 

[X] cc of abdominal fat was then placed through the right endaural incision around the prosthesis. Copious irrigation with vancomycin solution was used in the right surgical sites. Deep dermal sutures using 4-0 Monocryl were also placed in a buried interrupted fashion. The skin was closed superficially with 5-0 plain gut in a simple, running fashion. The right endaural incision was then closed with 3-0 deep vicryls for the muscular layer. Deep dermal sutures using 4-0 Monocryl were also placed in a buried fashion.
